task: Using Wikipedia, find the height of the Eiffel Tower and the roof height (not tip height) of the Empire State Building, both in metres, and report which structure is taller by that measure and by how many metres.

checked_claims.claim: Eiffel Tower roof height (architectural height) is 300 metres
checked_claims.observed: Wikipedia Eiffel Tower page shows: Architectural height = 300 m (984 ft), Tip height = 330 m (1,083 ft), Top floor = 276 m (906 ft)

checked_claims.claim: Empire State Building roof height is 380 metres
checked_claims.observed: Wikipedia Empire State Building page states: 'The building has a roof height of 1,250 feet (380 m) and stands a total of 1,454 feet (443.2 m) tall including its antenna'

checked_claims.claim: Empire State Building is taller than Eiffel Tower by 80 metres at roof height
checked_claims.observed: Empire State Building roof height 380m > Eiffel Tower roof height 300m. Difference = 80 metres

local_audit.engine: Holo-3.1-35B-A3B q4_k_m via llama.cpp (on-device, Apple Silicon)
local_audit.checked_observation: Empire State Building roof height 380m > Eiffel Tower roof height 300m. Difference = 80 metres
local_audit.result: No. The screenshot is about the Eiffel Tower, not the Empire State Building.
